Caption: Calculated convex hull analysis of C6, C4BN, C2B2N2 , and B4N4 networks from the C–B–N system. The experimentally known phase of g-C3N3 [36] is shown on the convex hull's vertex. The data points on the convex hull indicate the thermodynamically stable structures.
Calculated phonon band structure of (a) C6, (b) C4BN, (c) C2B2N2 and (d) B4N4 biphenylene networks.
Calculated HSE06 band structure of (a) C6, (b) C4BN, (c) C2B2N2 and (d) B4N4 biphenylene networks.
(a) Different adsorption sites of the biphenylene network and (b) Calculated Li (K) adsorption energy at the most favorable site.

Calculated diffusion pathways of Li atom (upper panel) and K atom (lower panel) on (a and d) C6, (b and e) C4BN, and (c and f) C2B2N2 networks, with corresponding calculated highest diffusion barrier.

(a) Calculated diffusion barriers and theoretical capacities of the biphenylene networks compared with other anode materials from literature [24,55–57]. Calculated voltage profile of Li(K) loading on (b) C 6 , (c) C 4 BN and (d) C 2 B 2 N 2 .
